transformation_type: alpha-methylenation of carbonyl compounds via aminomethylation and elimination
mechanism_type: enolate trapping with Eschenmoser salt followed by N-oxide / Hofmann / Cope-type elimination
common_conditions: generate enolate with strong base, add Eschenmoser salt, then eliminate dimethylamine under oxidative or basic conditions to reveal an exocyclic methylene
description_short: Method for converting enolizable carbonyl compounds into exocyclic methylene products by enolate capture of Eschenmoser’s salt followed by elimination from the resulting aminomethyl intermediate.

transformation_type: allylic alcohol to gamma,delta-unsaturated amide rearrangement
mechanism_type: Claisen-type [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement through ketene N,O-acetal / ketene aminal intermediates
common_conditions: heat allylic or benzylic alcohol with N,N-dimethylacetamide dimethyl acetal at elevated temperature to induce [3,3]-rearrangement to an unsaturated amide
description_short: Claisen-type rearrangement that converts allylic alcohols into gamma,delta-unsaturated amides under thermal conditions using DMA-DMA.

transformation_type: fragmentation of epoxyketone hydrazones to alkynones / alkynals
mechanism_type: Grob-type fragmentation of alpha,beta-epoxy ketone hydrazones with loss of nitrogen and sulfinate
common_conditions: prepare hydrazone of epoxyketone, then trigger fragmentation by base, acid, or heat to generate cyclic or acyclic alkynones / alkynals
description_short: Fragmentation of epoxy ketone hydrazones that furnishes alkynones or alkynals through a nitrogen-expelling Grob-type process.

mechanism_type: iminium formation with formaldehyde followed by hydride transfer from formic acid
common_conditions: combine primary or secondary amine with formaldehyde and formic acid under heating to obtain selectively N-methylated amine
description_short: One-pot reductive methylation of primary or secondary amines using formaldehyde and formic acid, often without overalkylation to quaternary ammonium salts.

mechanism_type: boron-enolate aldol addition through a Zimmerman-Traxler transition state
common_conditions: generate Z-boron enolate of N-acyl oxazolidinone with dibutylboron triflate and base, then add aldehyde at low temperature and perform oxidative workup
synonym_names: Evans boron aldol reaction|Evans auxiliary aldol
description_short: Highly diastereoselective syn aldol reaction of chiral N-acyl oxazolidinones with aldehydes through Z-boron enolates and Zimmerman-Traxler transition states.
